result.claim: A unique database with more than 980 magnetic nanoparticles was assembled.
result.original_text: a unique database with more than 980 magnetic nanoparticles collected from scientific articles is assembled

result.claim: Several tree-based ensemble models were trained to predict SAR, r1 and r2 relaxivity.
result.original_text: several tree-based ensemble models are trained to predict SAR, r1 and r2 relaxivity

result.claim: After hyperparameter optimization, models reach performances of R2 = 0.86, R2 = 0.78, and R2 = 0.75, respectively.
result.original_text: After hyperparameter optimization, models reach performances of R2 = 0.86, R2 = 0.78, and R2 = 0.75, respectively

result.claim: Testing the models on samples unseen during the training shows no performance drops.
result.original_text: Testing the models on samples unseen during the training shows no performance drops

result.claim: The initial database was manually collected from 126 unique scientific articles.
result.original_text: we manually collected 1282 raw samples from 126 unique scientific articles

result.claim: The initial database contained 1282 raw samples.

result.claim: The dataset includes parameters such as chemical composition, shape, length, width, depth, zeta potential, and hydrodynamic radius.
result.original_text: Those include chemical composition of core, shape, length, width, depth, zeta potential, hydrodynamic radius

result.claim: The dataset includes magnetic parameters measured by SQUID under 300 K.
result.original_text: magnetic parameters of nanoparticles measured by SQUID under 300 K

result.claim: The dataset includes parameters of magnetic field during experiments.
result.original_text: parameters of magnetic field during experiments

result.claim: The dataset includes concentration of nanoparticles.
result.original_text: concentration of nanoparticles

result.claim: The dataset includes efficacy metrics SAR value for hyperthermia and r1 and r2 relaxivities for MRI.
result.original_text: efficacy of nanomagnets in hyperthermia treatment (SAR value) and MRI (r1 and r2 relaxivities)

result.claim: Engineered features include average magnetic moment of metals in the core.
result.original_text: Average magnetic moment of metals in the core was calculated

result.claim: Engineered features include average spin of metal elements on the surface.
result.original_text: Average spin of metal elements on the surface was obtained

result.claim: Engineered features include partition coefficient LogP and number of H acceptors for organic coating.
result.original_text: we employed a partition coefficient LogP and number of H acceptors

result.claim: Engineered features include volume and surface area calculated from shape and size.
result.original_text: Volume and surface area were calculated using the shape and size of the nanoparticle

result.claim: A Pearson correlation matrix was calculated and analyzed to investigate feature redundancy.
result.original_text: we calculated and analyzed the Pearson correlation matrix

result.claim: Geometrical features with high cross-correlations were reduced to a single area-volume feature.
result.original_text: a group of geometrical features with high cross-correlations and reduced them to a single area-volume feature

result.claim: The number of outliers and missing values was investigated to keep only the most informative features.
result.original_text: we investigated the number of outliers and missing values to keep only the most informative features

result.claim: After minimal data cleansing, the dataset contained more than 980 materials.
result.original_text: we obtained the dataset of more than 980 nanosized metals, bimetals, and metal oxides

result.claim: The final dataset includes more than 120 core compositions.
result.original_text: more than 120 core and 50 shell compositions

result.claim: The data was split into train and test sets with a 80/20 ratio.
result.original_text: splitting to train and test sets in the 80/20 ratio

result.claim: 211 samples were removed to ensure each remaining sample had no more than 15% missing values.
result.original_text: we first removed the total of 211 samples such that each sample in the remaining dataset had no more than 15% of missing values

result.claim: kNN imputation technique was used for parameters with less data sparsity.
result.original_text: we chose kNN imputation technique

result.claim: Random Forest and Gradient Boosting algorithms were used.
result.original_text: we opted for tree-based algorithms such as Random Forest and Gradient Boosting

result.claim: Hyperparameter tuning was done using the Optuna framework.
result.original_text: hyperparameter tuning was done using the model-agnostic Optuna framework

result.claim: For each regression problem, four models were optimized and compared.
result.original_text: For each regression problem, we optimized and compared performance of four models

result.claim: The best model was selected using mean Q2 and RMSE for tenfold cross-validation and R2 and RMSE for test samples.
result.original_text: The best model was selected using the following metrics: mean Q<sup>2</sup> and RMSE for tenfold cross-validation and R2 and RMSE for test samples
